key_finding: AGO4 tetrad = D589-E629-G671-R809; catalytic Asp669->Gly and His807->Arg (2 of 4 residues lost) vs active AGO2 D-E-D-H
context: Human AGO2/AGO3/AGO4 full-length UniProt sequences; NW alignment
confidence_limitations: High for the two clean ungapped substitutions; alignment is pairwise not full MSA

claim_tested: AGO4 has an annotated catalytic center
key_finding: UniProt annotates catalytic binding sites for AGO2 (597/669/807) and AGO3 (598/638/670/808) but NONE for AGO4 (or AGO1)
confidence_limitations: Database-level; reflects curator judgement not a direct assay

key_finding: 'a single mammalian family member, Argonaute2, being responsible for messenger RNA cleavage activity'
confidence_limitations: Seminal; predates full paralog surveys but conclusion holds

key_finding: 'Of the four human AGO paralogs, only AGO2 has been shown to have slicer activity. The others (AGO1, AGO3 and AGO4)...slicer-independent'; paper adds AGO3 (not AGO4) as active
context: Recombinant human AGO cleavage assays + AGO3 crystal structure
confidence_limitations: Directly addresses paralogs; AGO4 not shown active

key_finding: 'we have mutated inactive human Ago1 and Ago3 and generated catalytic Argonaute proteins'; N-terminal elements + PIWI center needed
confidence_limitations: Shows AGO1/AGO3 inactive as wild type; AGO4 not engineered/rescued here

key_finding: All four AGO1-AGO4 recruit TNRC6/GW182 which silence via translational repression and deadenylation independent of Argonaute catalysis
